mapping.a_element: number of distinct tags in the system (global/local vocabulary size)
mapping.b_element: average number of distinct sites visited (territory explored) by the N walkers

mapping.a_element: the 'suitably defined notion of time' driving vocabulary growth

brief_justification: Both merely track a count of distinct entities over a time-like variable, but the driving machinery differs: paper A observes sub-linear power-law (Heaps'-type) growth attributed to collaborative/cognitive innovation processes, whereas paper B's distinct-site count arises from N independent random walkers exploring a lattice/fractal, with asymptotics governed by an inverse-logarithmic series in N and by order statistics of exit/trapping times. There is no shared governing equation or role-for-role process, only the superficial 'number of distinct X' overlap and different functional forms (power law in t vs. series in ln^{-n}(N)).
